description_long: This object is a compact, freestanding clock tower with a rounded, arched top and a broad, rectangular base, constructed primarily from dark wood. The front features a large, circular clock face with black Arabic numerals, two black hands, and a smaller subdial above the center, all set against a beige background. The clock face is bordered by a metallic ring, adding a subtle decorative accent. The sides are smooth and unadorned, emphasizing the solid, monolithic form of the structure. The rear and top views reveal a hollowed-out interior, suggesting the object is not fully solid. The overall design is simple and functional, with minimal ornamentation, evoking early 20th-century or utilitarian public building styles.
description: A dark wooden, arched clock tower with a large beige clock face, black numerals, and a sturdy rectangular base. The design is simple, with a metallic ring accent and a hollowed interior.
description_view_0: The front view displays the prominent circular clock face with black Arabic numerals, two hands, a subdial, and a metallic border set into the arched wooden structure.
description_view_1: The side view shows the smooth, curved wooden profile and the thickness of the structure, with no visible features except the arch and base.
description_view_2: The top view reveals the hollowed-out interior of the tower, showing the circular opening and the thickness of the wooden walls.
description_view_3: The opposite side view mirrors the first, showing the same smooth, curved wooden profile and solid base.
attribute_clock_face_design: The clock face is circular with a beige background, black Arabic numerals for each hour, two black hands (hour and minute), and a smaller subdial above the center. The face is bordered by a thin metallic ring, and there are no additional decorative elements.
